hazard.summary: 429 listed in retryableStatusCodes alongside 502/503/504. All receive pure exponential backoff capped at maxDelayMs: 5000ms. Retry-After header is never read. A quota exhaustion 429 with Retry-After: 10800 (3 hours) retries after 5 seconds, hits the same wall, retries again, and exhausts all attempts — never surfacing the real condition.
signals: calculateDelay() applied to all retryable statuses identically
Retry-After header never read in fetchWithRetry()
quota exhaustion retried every 5 seconds until maxRetries exhausted
real condition (3-hour quota exhaustion) never surfaced to caller

constraints: 429 must not be listed alongside 5xx in a shared retryableStatusCodes array — taxonomy collapse guarantees misclassification.
Retry-After must be inspected before any backoff calculation is applied.
maxDelayMs must not override Retry-After — server-directed wait is authoritative.
Retry-After values exceeding a threshold must classify the response as STOP and surface immediately, not consume retry attempts.
The fix that parses Retry-After for delay timing does not constitute correct classification — WAIT vs STOP still requires explicit decisioning.
knobs: maxRetryAfterSeconds (60s default in fix) — threshold for surfacing vs honoring
retryableStatusCodes — must not include 429 without separate handling path
calculateDelay() — must check for Retry-After before applying exponential backoff
maxDelayMs — must be subordinate to Retry-After, not override it
verification: Configure any LLM provider with quota-based limits
Verify Retry-After is read before backoff is applied
Verify response is surfaced as quota exhaustion, not retried
Verify maxDelayMs does not truncate large Retry-After values
notes: Filed via static analysis (pitstop-scanner). Core runtime location: packages/runtime/src/util/retry-utils.ts. Fix merged in PR #3842 — 178 additions, 4 commits, tests added for Retry-After parsing and 429 branch. The fix is correct but incomplete: throw if Retry-After > 60s implements 'avoid hanging' not 'make correct decision.' WAIT vs STOP decisioning is still absent — the runtime does not distinguish transient throttle from quota exhaustion, only avoids blocking indefinitely. This is the same class as PT-2026-04-16 copilot-cli (signal_failure_type: overridden) but in a core AI agent runtime rather than a CLI transport layer. CopilotKit is developer-facing agent infrastructure — this failure propagates to every downstream consumer of the runtime.

constraints: 429 must not share retry path with 5xx
Retry-After is authoritative — maxDelayMs must not override it
STOP classification required when Retry-After exceeds threshold
anti_patterns: exponential backoff applied before header inspection
quota exhaustion retried until attempts exhausted
